{
  "judge_id": "...",
  "active_set_recognized": ["R1.4", "R1.7", "RX.1", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "scores": {
    "R1.4": 4,
    "R1.7": 5,
    "RX.1": 5,
    "RX.3": 5,
    "RX.4": 3,
    "RX.5": 4
  },
  "rationale": {
    "R1.4": "The output identifies and articulates the delta between CalibSynth and standard code-generation pipelines, mapping against at least three prior works.",
    "R1.7": "The output audits every load-bearing citation with bibliographic precision.",
    "RX.1": "The output marks confidence for each recommendation and identifies missing evidence.",
    "RX.3": "The output covers exactly the agreed J*(d).",
    "RX.4": "While the output is harm-free, it does not identify harmful patterns in the original.",
    "RX.5": "Every non-cosmetic change is annotated with source and reason."
  },
  "confidence": {
    "R1.4": 0.9,
    "R1.7": 0.95,
    "RX.1": 0.95,
    "RX.3": 1,
    "RX.4": 0.8,
    "RX.5": 0.9
  },
  "blockers": []
}

rationale.R1.4: The output identifies and articulates the delta between CalibSynth and standard code-generation pipelines, mapping against at least three prior works.
rationale.R1.7: The output audits every load-bearing citation with bibliographic precision.
rationale.RX.1: The output marks confidence for each recommendation and identifies missing evidence.
rationale.RX.3: The output covers exactly the agreed J*(d).
rationale.RX.4: While the output is harm-free, it does not identify harmful patterns in the original.
rationale.RX.5: Every non-cosmetic change is annotated with source and reason.
